objective: Move spatial random sampling off the rejected EGL-mapped NV12_ER scratch path: copy the mapped input sequentially into persistent CUDA-owned pitch-linear NV12 buffers, run VPI CUDA PerspectiveWarp on those owned buffers, then copy the result sequentially back to the mapped output scratch before NvBufSurfTransform and NVENC.

entry_context.project_positioning: Jetson Orin NX heterogeneous video compute and device-side dataflow optimization, with EIS as the representative workload.
entry_context.quality_anchor: resid_r15_s07 remains the accepted Regular-gate stabilization-strength anchor and is not changed in this loop.
previous_positive_evidence: cuda_double_surface_debug_v1 Test1 proved full-frame sequential CUDA copy between two EGL-mapped pitch-linear NV12_ER scratch surfaces is visually clean.
experiments/vpi_cuda_mem_warp proved VPI CUDA PerspectiveWarp works on CUDA-owned pitch-linear memory.
The accepted MMAPI/VPI/NVENC path proved source_to_dest PerspectiveWarp semantics and encoder-compatible output.
entry_context.previous_negative_evidence: Custom CUDA integer translate and affine random sampling over the current EGL-mapped NV12_ER scratch path tear even when basic full-frame copy is clean.
entry_context.root_hypothesis: Keep EGL-mapped surfaces only as sequential copy boundaries. Let persistent CUDA-owned buffers carry random access, and let VPI own PerspectiveWarp execution and synchronization.
entry_context.device_availability: The Jetson is disconnected for the current local session. Local work may prepare and validate source generation only; build, video correctness, and timing claims require a later Jetson run.

frozen_variables: Use the Regular05 H264 source from the existing MMAPI evidence path on Jetson
Run the MMAPI sample with num_files=1 only
Do not tune EIS quality parameters
Keep the block-linear main decode/encode chain and pitch-linear NV12_ER MMAPI scratch boundary
Do not run a custom random-sampling CUDA kernel on EGL-mapped scratch
Use persistent CUDA-owned input/output Y and UV allocations
Use persistent VPI CUDA wrappers and stream for the CUDA-owned buffers
Copy EGL input to CUDA-owned input sequentially before VPI
Copy CUDA-owned output to EGL output sequentially after VPI sync
No accepted acceleration claim before same-input Jetson timing and visual review

planned_evidence.accepted_comparison: accepted EGLImage PerspectiveWarp path using the same Regular05 source and resid_r15_s07 matrix

local_validation_result.remaining_blocker: Jetson is disconnected; real MMAPI/VPI/CUDA compilation, video correctness, manual review, and stage timing are pending.

non_identity.direct_nv12_dx8: lower-half/content corruption, ideal-reference MAE p95 about 35.235669
non_identity.direct_nv12_plane_size_fix: still corrupt, ideal-reference MAE p95 about 31.444161
non_identity.rgba_with_vpi_convert_back: black border clean but content/color error, ideal-reference MAE p95 about 51.682096
non_identity.rgba_with_custom_nv12_writeback: bridge-internal RGBA warp is correct, but final NV12/encoded output has large color/content error

standalone_rgba_vpi.meaning: VPI CUDA PerspectiveWarp over CUDA-owned RGBA pitch memory is correct by itself; the bridge failure is in the MMAPI return-to-NV12/NVENC portion.

jetson_result.claim_boundary: Do not claim this CUDA-owned bridge solves non-identity MMAPI device-stage warp, preserves resid_r15_s07, or provides acceleration. Accepted fallback remains the VPI-managed EGLImage/stream-only/NvBuffer pair path with low-single-digit measured gains.

identity_gate: no green output, block tearing, unrelated content, or color-channel corruption
translate_gate: coherent configured integer shift with no per-band or block-dependent displacement
difference against a software-generated translate reference is recorded
matrix_gate: manual visual review finds no regression against the accepted EGLImage path
black_border_p95 below 0.01 or explicitly classified as the known gray-threshold conditional case
performance_gate: same-input total device-stage timing is recorded
copy-in, VPI warp, copy-out, EGL registration, and NvBufSurfTransform timing are separated
accepted only if total stage running average improves by at least 10 percent over the same-run accepted EGLImage baseline without visual regression
